summary: Tested overvaluation scoring fix: MRVL correctly moved from Hot/Buy to Overpriced/Hold. 463 overvalued stocks now in Overpriced category. Hot/Bullish stocks have reasonable upside (no stocks with upside < -25%). Entry status includes STALE_SETUP and BLOWN_STOP. All 12 backend tests pass. Frontend working correctly.

issues: Overpriced category (463 stocks) is combined with Bearish (14 stocks) into single 'Bearish (477)' tab - this is a design choice, not a bug

critical_code_review_comments: Overvaluation penalty correctly applied: stocks with upside < -25% get score penalty and blocked from 'Buy'
MRVL correctly categorized: price=$87.84, intrinsic=$22.69, upside=-76.1% → Overpriced/Hold
463 overvalued stocks moved to Overpriced category (was previously in Hot/Bullish)
Hot stocks now have genuine upside (no stocks with upside < -25%)
Entry status classification includes STALE_SETUP (>10% price drift) and BLOWN_STOP (price at/below stop)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Overvaluation scoring fix verified. Key findings: (1) MRVL correctly in Overpriced category with Hold signal (was previously Hot/Buy with -76% upside). (2) 463 overvalued stocks moved to Overpriced. (3) Hot/Bullish categories now have stocks with reasonable upside only. (4) Entry status includes STALE_SETUP and BLOWN_STOP. (5) Frontend combines Overpriced+Bearish into single 'Bearish' tab (design choice).
